papers.title: RAGEN-2: Reasoning Collapse in Agentic RL
papers.abstract: Abstract Research identifies template collapse in multi-turn LLM agents as a hidden failure mode undetectable by entropy, proposing mutual information proxies and SNR-aware filtering to improve reasoning quality and task performance.  AI-generated summary RL training of multi-turn LLM agents is inherently unstable, and reasoning quality directly determines task performance. Entropy is widely used to track reasoning stability. However, entropy only measures diversity within the same input, and cannot tell whether reasoning actually responds to different inputs. In RAGEN-2, we find that even with stable entropy, models can rely on fixed templates that look diverse but are input-agnostic. We call this template collapse, a failure mode invisible to entropy and all existing metrics. To diagnose this failure, we decompose reasoning quality into within-input diversity (Entropy) and cross-input distinguishability (Mutual Information, MI), and introduce a family of mutual information proxies for online diagnosis. Across diverse tasks, mutual information correlates with final performance much more strongly than entropy, making it a more reliable proxy for reasoning quality. We further explain template collapse with a signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) mechanism. Low reward variance weakens task gradients, letting regularization terms dominate and erase cross-input reasoning differences. To address this, we propose SNR-Aware Filtering to select high-signal prompts per iteration using reward variance as a lightweight proxy. Across planning, math reasoning, web navigation, and code execution, the method consistently improves both input dependence and task performance.

ru.title: Взаимная информация вместо энтропии: диагностика скрытых сбоев в мультиагентных системах
ru.desc: Исследование выявляет коллапс шаблонов в многошаговых агентах на основе LLM как скрытый режим отказа, который не обнаруживается энтропией. Авторы предлагают использовать взаимную информацию для диагностики этого явления, когда модель использует фиксированные шаблоны ответов, не зависящие от входных данных. Они объясняют проблему механизмом отношения сигнала к шуму и предлагают метод SNR-Aware Filtering для отбора высокоинформативных примеров на каждой итерации обучения. Экспериментальные результаты на задачах планирования, математического рассуждения, веб-навигации и исполнения кода показывают, что предложенный подход улучшает как зависимость от входа, так и итоговую производительность агента.

en.title: Unveiling Template Collapse: Enhancing LLM Reasoning with Mutual Information
en.desc: This paper discusses a problem called template collapse in multi-turn large language model (LLM) agents, where models generate seemingly diverse outputs that do not actually respond to different inputs. The authors argue that traditional measures like entropy fail to detect this issue, as they only assess diversity within the same input rather than across different inputs. To better evaluate reasoning quality, they introduce mutual information proxies and a signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) mechanism, which help identify when models are relying on fixed templates. Their proposed SNR-Aware Filtering method enhances task performance by selecting prompts that provide high signal, leading to improved reasoning and input dependence across various tasks.

zh.title: 提升推理质量，解决模板崩溃问题
zh.desc: 本研究发现多轮大语言模型（LLM）代理中的模板崩溃是一种隐藏的失败模式，无法通过熵检测。我们提出了互信息代理和信噪比（SNR）感知过滤，以提高推理质量和任务表现。熵只能衡量同一输入的多样性，而无法判断推理是否真正响应不同的输入。通过引入互信息，我们能够更可靠地诊断推理质量，并在多种任务中显著提升输入依赖性和任务表现。

papers.title: Think in Strokes, Not Pixels: Process-Driven Image Generation via Interleaved Reasoning
papers.abstract: Abstract Process-driven image generation decomposes synthesis into iterative steps involving textual planning, visual drafting, textual reflection, and visual refinement, with step-wise supervision ensuring consistency and interpretability.  AI-generated summary Humans paint images incrementally: they plan a global layout, sketch a coarse draft, inspect, and refine details, and most importantly, each step is grounded in the evolving visual states. However, can unified multimodal models trained on text-image interleaved datasets also imagine the chain of intermediate states? In this paper, we introduce process-driven image generation, a multi-step paradigm that decomposes synthesis into an interleaved reasoning trajectory of thoughts and actions. Rather than generating images in a single step, our approach unfolds across multiple iterations, each consisting of 4 stages: textual planning, visual drafting, textual reflection, and visual refinement. The textual reasoning explicitly conditions how the visual state should evolve, while the generated visual intermediate in turn constrains and grounds the next round of textual reasoning. A core challenge of process-driven generation stems from the ambiguity of intermediate states: how can models evaluate each partially-complete image? We address this through dense, step-wise supervision that maintains two complementary constraints: for the visual intermediate states, we enforce the spatial and semantic consistency; for the textual intermediate states, we preserve the prior visual knowledge while enabling the model to identify and correct prompt-violating elements. This makes the generation process explicit, interpretable, and directly supervisable. To validate proposed method, we conduct experiments under various text-to-image generation benchmarks.

ru.title: Генерация изображений через итеративный процесс рассуждения и уточнения
ru.desc: В работе предложен метод генерации изображений на основе итеративного процесса, который разлагает синтез на несколько этапов: текстовое планирование, визуальное черновое наброски, текстовое размышление и визуальное уточнение. Модель работает как человек, создавая изображение постепенно, где текстовое рассуждение направляет эволюцию визуального состояния, а промежуточные изображения, в свою очередь, ограничивают и уточняют следующее текстовое рассуждение. Для обучения модели используется пошаговая супервизия, которая обеспечивает пространственную и семантическую согласованность промежуточных визуальных состояний, а также сохранение визуального знания в текстовых состояниях. Предложенный подход делает процесс генерации прозрачным, интерпретируемым и поддающимся прямому контролю через набор данных с чередованием текста и изображений.

en.title: Iterative Image Creation: Planning, Drafting, Reflecting, Refining
en.desc: This paper presents a new method for generating images using a process-driven approach that breaks down the creation into multiple steps. It involves four main stages: planning the image with text, creating a rough visual draft, reflecting on the draft with more text, and refining the visual details. The method ensures that each step is guided by the previous visual state and textual input, allowing for better consistency and interpretability. The authors tackle the challenge of evaluating incomplete images by using detailed supervision to maintain both visual and textual coherence throughout the generation process.

zh.title: 过程驱动的图像生成：逐步迭代，明确可控
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种过程驱动的图像生成方法，将合成过程分解为多个迭代步骤，包括文本规划、视觉草图、文本反思和视觉细化。每个步骤都通过逐步监督确保一致性和可解释性，使得生成过程更加明确。我们的方法通过多次迭代生成图像，而不是一次性完成，确保每个阶段的视觉状态与文本推理相互影响。实验结果表明，该方法在各种文本到图像生成基准测试中表现良好。

papers.title: MARS: Enabling Autoregressive Models Multi-Token Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract MARS is a fine-tuning method that enables autoregressive language models to predict multiple tokens per forward pass without architectural changes, maintaining accuracy while improving throughput and supporting dynamic speed adjustment.  AI-generated summary Autoregressive (AR) language models generate text one token at a time, even when consecutive tokens are highly predictable given earlier context. We introduce MARS (Mask AutoRegreSsion), a lightweight fine-tuning method that teaches an instruction-tuned AR model to predict multiple tokens per forward pass. MARS adds no architectural modifications, no extra parameters, and produces a single model that can still be called exactly like the original AR model with no performance degradation. Unlike speculative decoding, which maintains a separate draft model alongside the target, or multi-head approaches such as Medusa, which attach additional prediction heads, MARS requires only continued training on existing instruction data. When generating one token per forward pass, MARS matches or exceeds the AR baseline on six standard benchmarks. When allowed to accept multiple tokens per step, it maintains baseline-level accuracy while achieving 1.5-1.7x throughput. We further develop a block-level KV caching strategy for batch inference, achieving up to 1.71x wall-clock speedup over AR with KV cache on Qwen2.5-7B. Finally, MARS supports real-time speed adjustment via confidence thresholding: under high request load, the serving system can increase throughput on the fly without swapping models or restarting, providing a practical latency-quality knob for deployment.

ru.title: Генерируем быстрее: несколько токенов за один проход без переархитектурирования
ru.desc: MARS — это лёгкий метод fine-tuning для авторегрессивных языковых моделей, который позволяет предсказывать несколько токенов за один forward pass без изменения архитектуры. Метод требует только дополнительную тренировку на существующих данных инструкций и не добавляет новые параметры или компоненты. При предсказании одного токена за проход MARS сохраняет точность базовой модели, а при предсказании нескольких токенов достигает ускорения пропускной способности в 1.5-1.7 раза. Кроме того, MARS поддерживает динамическую адаптацию скорости генерации в реальном времени через пороговую фильтрацию уверенности, что делает его практичным решением для развёртывания под различными нагрузками.

en.title: MARS: Boosting Autoregressive Models with Multi-Token Predictions
en.desc: MARS is a fine-tuning technique designed for autoregressive language models that allows them to predict multiple tokens in a single forward pass. This method does not require any changes to the model's architecture or additional parameters, ensuring that the original model's performance remains intact. By leveraging MARS, models can achieve a significant increase in throughput, with reported improvements of 1.5-1.7 times compared to traditional methods. Additionally, MARS introduces a block-level key-value caching strategy for efficient batch inference and supports dynamic speed adjustments based on system load, enhancing real-time performance.

zh.title: MARS：提升自回归模型的效率与灵活性
zh.desc: MARS是一种微调方法，旨在使自回归语言模型在不改变架构的情况下，每次前向传播预测多个标记。该方法通过继续在现有指令数据上训练，避免了额外的参数和架构修改，保持了模型的准确性。MARS在生成单个标记时，能够在六个标准基准上匹配或超过自回归基线，而在每步接受多个标记时，提升了1.5-1.7倍的吞吐量。它还支持实时速度调整，能够在高请求负载下动态提高吞吐量，提供了一个实用的延迟-质量调节机制。

papers.title: SEVerA: Verified Synthesis of Self-Evolving Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract Formally Guarded Generative Models enable safe and correct agentic code generation by combining formal specifications with soft objectives, ensuring reliability in autonomous agent systems.  AI-generated summary Recent advances have shown the effectiveness of self-evolving LLM agents on tasks such as program repair and scientific discovery. In this paradigm, a planner LLM synthesizes an agent program that invokes parametric models, including LLMs, which are then tuned per task to improve performance. However, existing self-evolving agent frameworks provide no formal guarantees of safety or correctness. Because such programs are often executed autonomously on unseen inputs, this lack of guarantees raises reliability and security concerns. We formulate agentic code generation as a constrained learning problem, combining hard formal specifications with soft objectives capturing task utility. We introduce Formally Guarded Generative Models (FGGM), which allow the planner LLM to specify a formal output contract for each generative model call using first-order logic. Each FGGM call wraps the underlying model in a rejection sampler with a verified fallback, ensuring every returned output satisfies the contract for any input and parameter setting. Building on FGGM, we present SEVerA (Self-Evolving Verified Agents), a three-stage framework: Search synthesizes candidate parametric programs containing FGGM calls; Verification proves correctness with respect to hard constraints for all parameter values, reducing the problem to unconstrained learning; and Learning applies scalable gradient-based optimization, including GRPO-style fine-tuning, to improve the soft objective while preserving correctness. We evaluate SEVerA on Dafny program verification, symbolic math synthesis, and policy-compliant agentic tool use (τ^2-bench). Across tasks, SEVerA achieves zero constraint violations while improving performance over unconstrained and SOTA baselines, showing that formal behavioral constraints not only guarantee correctness but also steer synthesis toward higher-quality agents.

ru.title: Формальные гарантии безопасности для самообучающихся LLM-агентов
ru.desc: Статья представляет Formally Guarded Generative Models (FGGM) — метод, объединяющий формальные спецификации с мягкими целевыми функциями для безопасной генерации кода автономными агентами. Авторы предлагают фреймворк SEVerA, который использует rejection sampling с верифицированными fallback'ами для гарантии того, что каждый вывод генеративной модели удовлетворяет формальному контракту. Подход состоит из трёх этапов: синтез кандидатов программ, верификация корректности относительно жёстких ограничений и обучение через градиентную оптимизацию. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что SEVerA достигает нулевых нарушений ограничений при улучшении качества синтезируемых агентов.

en.title: Ensuring Safe and Reliable Code Generation for Autonomous Agents
en.desc: This paper introduces Formally Guarded Generative Models (FGGM) to enhance the safety and correctness of code generated by autonomous agents. By combining formal specifications with soft objectives, FGGM ensures that the generated code adheres to strict contracts defined using first-order logic. The authors present a framework called SEVerA, which includes stages for searching, verifying, and learning to create reliable agent programs. The results demonstrate that SEVerA can produce high-quality agents that meet formal constraints while improving performance on various tasks.

zh.title: 安全可靠的智能代码生成
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种形式化保护生成模型（FGGM），用于安全和正确的智能代码生成。通过结合硬性形式规范和软性目标，FGGM确保了自主代理系统的可靠性。我们还介绍了自我进化验证代理（SEVerA）框架，该框架通过搜索、验证和学习三个阶段来合成和优化代理程序。实验结果表明，SEVerA在多个任务中实现了零约束违规，同时提高了性能，证明了形式行为约束不仅保证了正确性，还能引导合成更高质量的代理。

papers.title: INSPATIO-WORLD: A Real-Time 4D World Simulator via Spatiotemporal Autoregressive Modeling
papers.abstract: Abstract INSPATIO-WORLD presents a real-time framework for generating high-fidelity dynamic scenes from single videos using spatiotemporal autoregressive architecture and joint distribution matching distillation.  AI-generated summary Building world models with spatial consistency and real-time interactivity remains a fundamental challenge in computer vision. Current video generation paradigms often struggle with a lack of spatial persistence and insufficient visual realism, making it difficult to support seamless navigation in complex environments. To address these challenges, we propose INSPATIO-WORLD, a novel real-time framework capable of recovering and generating high-fidelity, dynamic interactive scenes from a single reference video. At the core of our approach is a Spatiotemporal Autoregressive (STAR) architecture, which enables consistent and controllable scene evolution through two tightly coupled components: Implicit Spatiotemporal Cache aggregates reference and historical observations into a latent world representation, ensuring global consistency during long-horizon navigation; Explicit Spatial Constraint Module enforces geometric structure and translates user interactions into precise and physically plausible camera trajectories. Furthermore, we introduce Joint Distribution Matching Distillation (JDMD). By using real-world data distributions as a regularizing guide, JDMD effectively overcomes the fidelity degradation typically caused by over-reliance on synthetic data. Extensive experiments demonstrate that INSPATIO-WORLD significantly outperforms existing state-of-the-art (SOTA) models in spatial consistency and interaction precision, ranking first among real-time interactive methods on the WorldScore-Dynamic benchmark, and establishing a practical pipeline for navigating 4D environments reconstructed from monocular videos.

ru.title: Построение согласованных интерактивных миров из одного видео в реальном времени
ru.desc: INSPATIO-WORLD — это система для создания реалистичных динамических сцен из одного видео в реальном времени, основанная на пространственно-временной авторегрессивной архитектуре. Ядром подхода являются два компонента: неявный пространственно-временной кэш для агрегации данных и явный модуль пространственных ограничений для обеспечения физической правдоподобности. Авторы предлагают метод совместного распределения (JDMD) для дистилляции модели, используя реальные данные в качестве регуляризации, что решает проблему деградации качества из-за синтетических данных. Метод достигает лучших результатов по пространственной согласованности и точности взаимодействия на бенчмарке WorldScore-Dynamic среди методов реального времени.

en.title: Real-Time Dynamic Scene Generation from Single Videos
en.desc: INSPATIO-WORLD is a cutting-edge framework designed to create realistic and interactive dynamic scenes from a single video in real-time. It utilizes a Spatiotemporal Autoregressive (STAR) architecture that ensures spatial consistency and allows for user interactions to influence scene evolution. The framework incorporates an Implicit Spatiotemporal Cache for maintaining a coherent world representation and an Explicit Spatial Constraint Module for accurate camera movements. Additionally, Joint Distribution Matching Distillation (JDMD) is employed to enhance visual fidelity by aligning with real-world data distributions, leading to superior performance in interactive video generation tasks.

zh.title: 实时生成高保真动态场景的创新框架
zh.desc: INSPATIO-WORLD是一个实时框架，能够从单个视频生成高保真动态场景。它采用了时空自回归架构和联合分布匹配蒸馏技术，解决了计算机视觉中空间一致性和实时交互的挑战。该框架通过隐式时空缓存和显式空间约束模块，确保在长时间导航中场景的全球一致性和用户交互的精确性。实验结果表明，INSPATIO-WORLD在空间一致性和交互精度方面显著优于现有的最先进模型。

papers.title: FlowInOne:Unifying Multimodal Generation as Image-in, Image-out Flow Matching
papers.abstract: Abstract FlowInOne presents a vision-centric multimodal generation framework that unifies diverse input modalities into a single visual representation, enabling coherent image generation and editing through a unified flow matching model.  AI-generated summary Multimodal generation has long been dominated by text-driven pipelines where language dictates vision but cannot reason or create within it. We challenge this paradigm by asking whether all modalities, including textual descriptions, spatial layouts, and editing instructions, can be unified into a single visual representation. We present FlowInOne, a framework that reformulates multimodal generation as a purely visual flow, converting all inputs into visual prompts and enabling a clean image-in, image-out pipeline governed by a single flow matching model. This vision-centric formulation naturally eliminates cross-modal alignment bottlenecks, noise scheduling, and task-specific architectural branches, unifying text-to-image generation, layout-guided editing, and visual instruction following under one coherent paradigm. To support this, we introduce VisPrompt-5M, a large-scale dataset of 5 million visual prompt pairs spanning diverse tasks including physics-aware force dynamics and trajectory prediction, alongside VP-Bench, a rigorously curated benchmark assessing instruction faithfulness, spatial precision, visual realism, and content consistency. Extensive experiments demonstrate that FlowInOne achieves state-of-the-art performance across all unified generation tasks, surpassing both open-source models and competitive commercial systems, establishing a new foundation for fully vision-centric generative modeling where perception and creation coexist within a single continuous visual space.
affiliations: University of Electronic Science and Technology of China
University of Science and Technology of China

ru.title: Единый визуальный поток для мультимодальной генерации изображений
ru.desc: FlowInOne представляет инновационный подход к мультимодальной генерации, где все входные модальности — текст, пространственные разметки и инструкции редактирования — преобразуются в единое визуальное представление. Вместо традиционной текст-ориентированной архитектуры, авторы используют flow matching модель для создания единого визуального потока, что устраняет перекрёстные узкие места выравнивания модальностей. Для обучения и оценки предложены VisPrompt-5M — датасет из 5 миллионов пар визуальных подсказок и VP-Bench — бенчмарк для оценки верности инструкций и визуального качества. Эксперименты показывают, что модель достигает state-of-the-art результатов в генерации изображений, редактировании и визуальном следовании инструкциям, объединяя все задачи в одну когерентную парадигму.

en.title: Unifying Modalities for Seamless Image Generation
en.desc: FlowInOne is a new framework for multimodal generation that combines different types of inputs, like text and images, into one visual format. This approach allows for seamless image creation and editing by using a single flow matching model, which simplifies the process. By treating all inputs as visual prompts, FlowInOne avoids common issues found in traditional methods, such as misalignment between different modalities. The framework is supported by a large dataset and has shown to outperform existing models in various tasks, paving the way for a more integrated approach to generative modeling.

zh.title: 视觉驱动的多模态生成新纪元
zh.desc: FlowInOne是一个以视觉为中心的多模态生成框架，它将不同的输入模态统一为单一的视觉表示，从而实现连贯的图像生成和编辑。该框架通过一个统一的流匹配模型，将所有输入转换为视觉提示，形成一个干净的图像输入输出管道。FlowInOne消除了跨模态对齐的瓶颈和噪声调度，统一了文本到图像生成、布局引导编辑和视觉指令跟随等任务。通过引入VisPrompt-5M数据集和VP-Bench基准，FlowInOne在所有统一生成任务中实现了最先进的性能，建立了一个全新的视觉中心生成建模基础。

papers.title: TC-AE: Unlocking Token Capacity for Deep Compression Autoencoders
papers.abstract: Abstract TC-AE is a Vision Transformer-based architecture that improves deep compression autoencoders by addressing token space limitations and enhancing semantic structures through joint self-supervised training.  AI-generated summary We propose TC-AE, a ViT-based architecture for deep compression autoencoders. Existing methods commonly increase the channel number of latent representations to maintain reconstruction quality under high compression ratios. However, this strategy often leads to latent representation collapse, which degrades generative performance. Instead of relying on increasingly complex architectures or multi-stage training schemes, TC-AE addresses this challenge from the perspective of the token space, the key bridge between pixels and image latents, through two complementary innovations: Firstly, we study token number scaling by adjusting the patch size in ViT under a fixed latent budget, and identify aggressive token-to-latent compression as the key factor that limits effective scaling. To address this issue, we decompose token-to-latent compression into two stages, reducing structural information loss and enabling effective token number scaling for generation. Secondly, to further mitigate latent representation collapse, we enhance the semantic structure of image tokens via joint self-supervised training, leading to more generative-friendly latents. With these designs, TC-AE achieves substantially improved reconstruction and generative performance under deep compression. We hope our research will advance ViT-based tokenizer for visual generation.

ru.title: Сжатие изображений без потерь смысла: токенизация через многоэтапную компрессию
ru.desc: TC-AE — это архитектура на основе Vision Transformer для глубокой компрессии автокодировщиков изображений. Авторы решают проблему коллапса скрытых представлений, анализируя пространство токенов как ключевой мост между пикселями и скрытыми переменными. Они предлагают двухэтапное разложение компрессии токен-латент и улучшают семантическую структуру токенов через совместное самообучение. В результате метод достигает улучшенной реконструкции и производительности генерации при высоких коэффициентах сжатия.

en.title: Revolutionizing Deep Compression with TC-AE
en.desc: The paper introduces TC-AE, a new architecture based on Vision Transformers (ViT) that enhances deep compression autoencoders. It tackles the common issue of latent representation collapse, which occurs when increasing the channel number of latent representations to maintain quality during high compression. TC-AE innovatively adjusts the token space by scaling the number of tokens and optimizing patch sizes, which helps preserve structural information. Additionally, it employs joint self-supervised training to improve the semantic quality of image tokens, resulting in better reconstruction and generative performance under compression.

zh.title: TC-AE：提升深度压缩自编码器的生成性能
zh.desc: TC-AE是一种基于视觉变换器（ViT）的深度压缩自编码器架构，旨在解决现有方法在高压缩比下重建质量下降的问题。该方法通过调整补丁大小来研究令牌数量的缩放，从而在固定的潜在预算下实现有效的令牌到潜在表示的压缩。TC-AE还通过联合自监督训练增强图像令牌的语义结构，减少潜在表示崩溃的风险。最终，TC-AE在深度压缩下显著提高了重建和生成性能。

papers.title: Personalized RewardBench: Evaluating Reward Models with Human Aligned Personalization
papers.abstract: Abstract Personalized RewardBench evaluates reward models' ability to capture individual user preferences, revealing significant challenges in current models and demonstrating superior correlation with downstream performance compared to existing benchmarks.  AI-generated summary Pluralistic alignment has emerged as a critical frontier in the development of Large Language Models (LLMs), with reward models (RMs) serving as a central mechanism for capturing diverse human values. While benchmarks for general response quality are prevalent, evaluating how well reward models account for individual user preferences remains an open challenge. To bridge this gap, we introduce Personalized RewardBench, a novel benchmark designed to rigorously assess reward models' capacity to model personalized preferences. We construct chosen and rejected response pairs based on strict adherence to (or violation of) user-specific rubrics, ensuring that preference distinctions are uniquely tailored to the individual. In particular, human evaluations confirm that the primary discriminative factor between pairs is strictly personal preference, with both responses maintaining high general quality (e.g., correctness, relevance and helpfulness). Extensive testing reveals that existing state-of-the-art reward models struggle significantly with personalization, peaking at an accuracy of just 75.94%. Crucially, because an effective reward model benchmark should predict a reward model's performance on downstream tasks, we conduct experiments demonstrating that our benchmark exhibits a significantly higher correlation with downstream performance in both Best-of-N (BoN) sampling and Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) compared to existing baselines. These findings establish Personalized RewardBench as a robust and accurate proxy for evaluating reward models' performance in downstream applications.

ru.title: Персональные предпочтения: как научить модели вознаграждения понимать каждого пользователя
ru.desc: В статье представлена новая методика оценки моделей вознаграждения (reward models), которые используются для выравнивания LLM с разнообразными предпочтениями пользователей. Авторы создали бенчмарк Personalized RewardBench, содержащий пары ответов, составленные на основе индивидуальных критериев оценки каждого пользователя, чтобы различия в предпочтениях были строго персонализированы. Исследование показало, что современные модели вознаграждения плохо справляются с персонализацией, достигая максимальной точности всего 75.94%. Разработанный бенчмарк демонстрирует значительно более высокую корреляцию с производительностью на downstream задачах, таких как Best-of-N sampling и PPO, чем существующие альтернативы.

en.title: Personalized RewardBench: Tailoring AI to Individual Preferences
en.desc: The paper introduces Personalized RewardBench, a new benchmark for evaluating reward models in machine learning, specifically focusing on how well these models capture individual user preferences. It highlights the limitations of current reward models, which struggle to accurately reflect personalized preferences, achieving only 75.94% accuracy. The benchmark is designed using chosen and rejected response pairs that adhere to user-specific criteria, ensuring that evaluations are tailored to individual preferences. The results show that Personalized RewardBench correlates better with downstream performance in tasks like Best-of-N sampling and Proximal Policy Optimization than existing benchmarks, making it a valuable tool for assessing reward models.

zh.title: 个性化奖励模型评估的新标准
zh.desc: 个性化奖励基准（Personalized RewardBench）旨在评估奖励模型捕捉个体用户偏好的能力。当前的奖励模型在个性化方面面临重大挑战，且与下游任务的表现相关性较低。我们通过构建基于用户特定标准的选择和拒绝响应对，来严格评估奖励模型的个性化能力。实验结果表明，个性化奖励基准在预测奖励模型在下游任务中的表现方面，优于现有的基准。

papers.title: Neural Computers
papers.abstract: Abstract Neural Computers represent a new computing paradigm where models function as runtime systems, learning to execute tasks through I/O traces rather than explicit programming.  AI-generated summary We propose a new frontier: Neural Computers (NCs) -- an emerging machine form that unifies computation, memory, and I/O in a learned runtime state. Unlike conventional computers, which execute explicit programs, agents, which act over external execution environments, and world models, which learn environment dynamics, NCs aim to make the model itself the running computer. Our long-term goal is the Completely Neural Computer (CNC): the mature, general-purpose realization of this emerging machine form, with stable execution, explicit reprogramming, and durable capability reuse. As an initial step, we study whether early NC primitives can be learned solely from collected I/O traces, without instrumented program state. Concretely, we instantiate NCs as video models that roll out screen frames from instructions, pixels, and user actions (when available) in CLI and GUI settings. These implementations show that learned runtimes can acquire early interface primitives, especially I/O alignment and short-horizon control, while routine reuse, controlled updates, and symbolic stability remain open. We outline a roadmap toward CNCs around these challenges. If overcome, CNCs could establish a new computing paradigm beyond today's agents, world models, and conventional computers.

ru.title: Нейронный компьютер как самообучающаяся исполнительная система
ru.desc: Авторы предлагают концепцию Нейронных Компьютеров — нового типа машин, где модель глубокого обучения сама становится рабочей системой, обучаясь выполнять задачи прямо из примеров взаимодействия пользователя с интерфейсом, вместо явного программирования. В качестве прототипа они реализуют нейронные компьютеры как видео-модели, которые предсказывают следующие кадры экрана на основе инструкций, пиксельных данных и действий пользователя в CLI и GUI приложениях. Модели успешно обучаются базовым примитивам управления интерфейсом, включая выравнивание ввода-вывода и краткосрочное управление, однако вопросы переиспользования кода, контролируемых обновлений и символической стабильности остаются открытыми. Авторы представляют дорожную карту развития полностью нейронных компьютеров и предполагают, что эта парадигма может превзойти существующие подходы с агентами и мировыми моделями.

en.title: Revolutionizing Computing with Neural Computers
en.desc: Neural Computers (NCs) are a novel approach to computing that integrates computation, memory, and input/output (I/O) into a single learned runtime system. Unlike traditional computers that rely on explicit programming, NCs learn to perform tasks by analyzing I/O traces. The ultimate goal is to develop a Completely Neural Computer (CNC) that can execute tasks reliably and adaptively, allowing for reprogramming and reuse of capabilities. Initial experiments demonstrate that NCs can learn basic interface functions from I/O data, paving the way for future advancements in this new computing paradigm.

zh.title: 神经计算机：未来计算的新范式
zh.desc: 神经计算机（Neural Computers）是一种新型计算范式，模型通过输入/输出（I/O）轨迹学习执行任务，而不是依赖于显式编程。与传统计算机不同，神经计算机将计算、内存和I/O统一为一个学习的运行状态。我们的目标是完全神经计算机（Completely Neural Computer），实现稳定执行、显式重编程和持久能力重用。初步研究表明，神经计算机可以仅通过收集的I/O轨迹学习早期的接口原语，展示了这一新型计算机的潜力。

papers.title: Fast Spatial Memory with Elastic Test-Time Training
papers.abstract: Abstract Elastic Test-Time Training with fast spatial memory enables efficient 4D reconstruction through multi-chunk adaptation while maintaining stability against catastrophic forgetting.  AI-generated summary Large Chunk Test-Time Training (LaCT) has shown strong performance on long-context 3D reconstruction, but its fully plastic inference-time updates remain vulnerable to catastrophic forgetting and overfitting. As a result, LaCT is typically instantiated with a single large chunk spanning the full input sequence, falling short of the broader goal of handling arbitrarily long sequences in a single pass. We propose Elastic Test-Time Training inspired by elastic weight consolidation, that stabilizes LaCT fast-weight updates with a Fisher-weighted elastic prior around a maintained anchor state. The anchor evolves as an exponential moving average of past fast weights to balance stability and plasticity. Based on this updated architecture, we introduce Fast Spatial Memory (FSM), an efficient and scalable model for 4D reconstruction that learns spatiotemporal representations from long observation sequences and renders novel view-time combinations. We pre-trained FSM on large-scale curated 3D/4D data to capture the dynamics and semantics of complex spatial environments. Extensive experiments show that FSM supports fast adaptation over long sequences and delivers high-quality 3D/4D reconstruction with smaller chunks and mitigating the camera-interpolation shortcut. Overall, we hope to advance LaCT beyond the bounded single-chunk setting toward robust multi-chunk adaptation, a necessary step for generalization to genuinely longer sequences, while substantially alleviating the activation-memory bottleneck.

ru.title: Упругая адаптация для стабильной 4D реконструкции из длинных видеопоследовательностей
ru.desc: Статья предлагает метод Elastic Test-Time Training для стабилизации обновления весов нейросети во время тестирования, вдохновленный методом elastic weight consolidation. Авторы вводят Fast Spatial Memory (FSM) — модель для 4D реконструкции, которая обучается на представлениях пространственно-временных сцен из длинных последовательностей наблюдений. Ключевая идея состоит в использовании якорного состояния и экспоненциального скользящего среднего для балансирования стабильности и пластичности при адаптации к новым данным. Метод позволяет эффективно обрабатывать длинные последовательности несколькими блоками вместо одного большого блока, снижая катастрофическое забывание и улучшая качество 3D/4D реконструкции.

en.title: Advancing 4D Reconstruction with Elastic Test-Time Training
en.desc: This paper introduces Elastic Test-Time Training (ETTT) to enhance the performance of Large Chunk Test-Time Training (LaCT) for 4D reconstruction tasks. ETTT employs a Fisher-weighted elastic prior to stabilize fast-weight updates, preventing issues like catastrophic forgetting and overfitting during inference. The proposed Fast Spatial Memory (FSM) model efficiently learns from long observation sequences, allowing for effective spatiotemporal representation and novel view rendering. The results demonstrate that FSM can adapt quickly over long sequences while maintaining high-quality reconstruction, paving the way for robust multi-chunk adaptation in machine learning applications.

zh.title: 弹性测试时训练：高效的4D重建新方法
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种弹性测试时训练方法，旨在提高4D重建的效率。通过快速空间记忆（FSM），该方法能够从长时间观察序列中学习时空表示，并生成新视角的时间组合。我们引入了一种基于Fisher加权的弹性先验，来稳定快速权重更新，避免灾难性遗忘和过拟合。实验表明，FSM在处理长序列时能够快速适应，并提供高质量的3D/4D重建。

papers.title: Improving Semantic Proximity in Information Retrieval through Cross-Lingual Alignment
papers.abstract: Abstract Multilingual retrieval models exhibit bias toward English documents in mixed-language document pools, which is addressed through a novel training strategy that improves cross-lingual alignment with minimal data.  AI-generated summary With the increasing accessibility and utilization of multilingual documents, Cross-Lingual Information Retrieval (CLIR) has emerged as an important research area. Conventionally, CLIR tasks have been conducted under settings where the language of documents differs from that of queries, and typically, the documents are composed in a single coherent language. In this paper, we highlight that in such a setting, the cross-lingual alignment capability may not be evaluated adequately. Specifically, we observe that, in a document pool where English documents coexist with another language, most multilingual retrievers tend to prioritize unrelated English documents over the related document written in the same language as the query. To rigorously analyze and quantify this phenomenon, we introduce various scenarios and metrics designed to evaluate the cross-lingual alignment performance of multilingual retrieval models. Furthermore, to improve cross-lingual performance under these challenging conditions, we propose a novel training strategy aimed at enhancing cross-lingual alignment. Using only a small dataset consisting of 2.8k samples, our method significantly improves the cross-lingual retrieval performance while simultaneously mitigating the English inclination problem. Extensive analyses demonstrate that the proposed method substantially enhances the cross-lingual alignment capabilities of most multilingual embedding models.
affiliations: Department of Computer Science and Engineering, Korea University

ru.title: Преодоление англоязычного смещения в многоязычном поиске
ru.desc: В работе исследуется проблема смещения многоязычных моделей поиска в сторону английских документов при работе с разноязычными коллекциями. Авторы предлагают новую стратегию обучения, которая улучшает кросс-лингвальное выравнивание эмбеддингов на минимальном наборе данных из 2.8k примеров. Метод значительно повышает качество поиска документов на других языках и снижает англоязычное смещение в многоязычных моделях. Подход демонстрирует универсальную эффективность при адаптации большинства существующих многоязычных моделей встраивания.

en.title: Enhancing Cross-Lingual Retrieval: Bridging Language Gaps with Minimal Data
en.desc: This paper addresses the bias in multilingual retrieval models that favor English documents when mixed-language document pools are used. It highlights the inadequacy of evaluating cross-lingual alignment in scenarios where English and other languages coexist. The authors propose a novel training strategy that enhances cross-lingual alignment with minimal data, specifically using a small dataset of 2.8k samples. Their method significantly improves retrieval performance and reduces the tendency to prioritize unrelated English documents over relevant ones in the same language as the query.

zh.title: 提升跨语言检索能力的新策略
zh.desc: 本论文探讨了多语言检索模型在混合语言文档池中对英语文档的偏见问题。我们提出了一种新颖的训练策略，旨在通过最小的数据量来改善跨语言对齐能力。研究表明，传统的多语言检索模型在处理包含英语和其他语言的文档时，往往优先选择与查询无关的英语文档。通过使用仅包含2800个样本的小数据集，我们的方法显著提高了跨语言检索性能，并有效减轻了对英语的偏向。

papers.title: Beyond Hard Negatives: The Importance of Score Distribution in Knowledge Distillation for Dense Retrieval
papers.abstract: Abstract Stratified sampling improves knowledge distillation by preserving the full range of teacher scores, outperforming traditional sampling methods in retrieval tasks.  AI-generated summary Transferring knowledge from a cross-encoder teacher via Knowledge Distillation (KD) has become a standard paradigm for training retrieval models. While existing studies have largely focused on mining hard negatives to improve discrimination, the systematic composition of training data and the resulting teacher score distribution have received relatively less attention. In this work, we highlight that focusing solely on hard negatives prevents the student from learning the comprehensive preference structure of the teacher, potentially hampering generalization. To effectively emulate the teacher score distribution, we propose a Stratified Sampling strategy that uniformly covers the entire score spectrum. Experiments on in-domain and out-of-domain benchmarks confirm that Stratified Sampling, which preserves the variance and entropy of teacher scores, serves as a robust baseline, significantly outperforming top-K and random sampling in diverse settings. These findings suggest that the essence of distillation lies in preserving the diverse range of relative scores perceived by the teacher.

ru.title: Стратифицированная выборка сохраняет многообразие знаний учителя
ru.desc: В статье предложен метод стратифицированной выборки для дистилляции знаний в задачах информационного поиска. Авторы показывают, что фокусировка только на сложных отрицательных примерах препятствует студент-модели научиться полной структуре предпочтений учителя. Новый подход равномерно охватывает весь спектр оценок учителя, сохраняя дисперсию и энтропию распределения. Экспериментальные результаты демонстрируют, что стратифицированная выборка значительно превосходит методы top-K и случайной выборки на множестве тестовых наборов.

en.title: Stratified Sampling: Unlocking the Full Potential of Knowledge Distillation
en.desc: This paper introduces a new method called Stratified Sampling to enhance Knowledge Distillation (KD) in machine learning. Traditional methods often focus on hard negatives, which can limit the student's ability to learn from the full range of teacher scores. By using Stratified Sampling, the authors ensure that the training data reflects the entire spectrum of scores from the teacher, leading to better generalization. Experiments show that this approach outperforms conventional sampling techniques, highlighting the importance of maintaining the diversity of teacher score distributions during the distillation process.

zh.title: 分层抽样：提升知识蒸馏效果的关键
zh.desc: 分层抽样通过保留教师评分的全范围，改善了知识蒸馏的效果，优于传统的抽样方法。知识蒸馏已成为训练检索模型的标准方法，主要通过跨编码器教师传递知识。现有研究多集中于挖掘困难负样本，而对训练数据的系统组成和教师评分分布关注较少。我们的研究表明，单纯关注困难负样本会阻碍学生学习教师的全面偏好结构，因此我们提出的分层抽样策略能够有效模拟教师评分分布，显著提升模型的泛化能力。

papers.title: DeonticBench: A Benchmark for Reasoning over Rules
papers.abstract: Abstract DEONTICBENCH presents a benchmark for evaluating large language models on complex, context-specific deontic reasoning tasks drawn from real-world legal and policy domains, supporting both symbolic and non-symbolic approaches to rule-based problem solving.  AI-generated summary Reasoning with complex, context-specific rules remains challenging for large language models (LLMs). In legal and policy settings, this manifests as deontic reasoning: reasoning about obligations, permissions, and prohibitions under explicit rules. While many recent benchmarks emphasize short-context mathematical reasoning, fewer focus on long-context, high-stakes deontic reasoning. To address this gap, we introduce DEONTICBENCH, a benchmark of 6,232 tasks across U.S. federal taxes, airline baggage policies, U.S. immigration administration, and U.S. state housing law. These tasks can be approached in multiple ways, including direct reasoning in language or with the aid of symbolic computation. Besides free-form chain-of-thought reasoning, DEONTICBENCH enables an optional solver-based workflow in which models translate statutes and case facts into executable Prolog, leading to formal problem interpretations and an explicit program trace. We release reference Prolog programs for all instances. Across frontier LLMs and coding models, best hard-subset performance reaches only 44.4% on SARA Numeric and 46.6 macro-F1 on Housing. We further study training with supervised fine-tuning and reinforcement learning for symbolic program generation. Although training improves Prolog generation quality, current RL methods still fail to solve these tasks reliably. Overall, DEONTICBENCH provides a benchmark for studying context-grounded rule reasoning in real-world domains under both symbolic and non-symbolic settings.
affiliations: Telecom Paris, Institut Polytechnique de Paris

ru.title: Когда правила сложнее слов: бенчмарк для деонтического мышления LLM
ru.desc: В статье представлен бенчмарк DEONTICBENCH для оценки способности больших языковых моделей к деонтическому рассуждению — логическому выводу об обязательствах, разрешениях и запретах в контексте реальных правовых и политических задач. Бенчмарк содержит 6232 задачи из областей налогообложения, авиационных правил, иммиграции и жилищного права США, которые можно решать как прямым языковым рассуждением, так и с помощью символических вычислений на Prolog. Исследование показывает, что современные LLM достигают лишь 44-47% точности на сложных подмножествах данных, даже при использовании fine-tuning и обучения с подкреплением для генерации символических программ. Работа демонстрирует значительный разрыв между возможностями текущих моделей и требованиями для надёжного решения контекстуально-зависимых задач с применением правил в реальных областях.

en.title: DEONTICBENCH: Advancing Deontic Reasoning in AI
en.desc: DEONTICBENCH is a new benchmark designed to evaluate large language models (LLMs) on complex reasoning tasks related to obligations and permissions in legal and policy contexts. It includes 6,232 tasks that cover various real-world scenarios, such as U.S. federal taxes and immigration laws. The benchmark allows for both direct language reasoning and the use of symbolic computation, enabling models to translate legal texts into executable Prolog code. Despite improvements from supervised fine-tuning and reinforcement learning, current models still struggle to achieve high accuracy on these challenging tasks.

zh.title: DEONTICBENCH：推动复杂规则推理的基准
zh.desc: DEONTICBENCH是一个用于评估大型语言模型在复杂的、特定上下文的义务推理任务上的基准，任务来源于真实的法律和政策领域。该基准包含6232个任务，涵盖美国联邦税务、航空行李政策、移民管理和州住房法等领域。研究表明，尽管通过监督微调和强化学习可以提高符号程序生成的质量，但现有的强化学习方法仍然无法可靠地解决这些任务。DEONTICBENCH为研究现实世界领域中的上下文基础规则推理提供了一个重要的基准。

papers.title: Learning to Hint for Reinforcement Learning
papers.abstract: Abstract HiLL is a reinforcement learning framework that adaptively generates hints conditioned on reasoner errors to improve learning signals and transfer performance in group relative policy optimization.  AI-generated summary Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) is widely used for reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards, but it often suffers from advantage collapse: when all rollouts in a group receive the same reward, the group yields zero relative advantage and thus no learning signal. For example, if a question is too hard for the reasoner, all sampled rollouts can be incorrect and receive zero reward. Recent work addresses this issue by adding hints or auxiliary scaffolds to such hard questions so that the reasoner produces mixed outcomes and recovers a non-zero update. However, existing hints are usually fixed rather than adapted to the current reasoner, and a hint that creates learning signal under the hinted input does not necessarily improve the no-hint policy used at test time. To this end, we propose Hint Learning for Reinforcement Learning (HiLL), a framework that jointly trains a hinter policy and a reasoner policy during RL. For each hard question, the hinter generates hints online conditioned on the current reasoner's incorrect rollout, allowing hint generation to adapt to the reasoner's evolving errors. We further introduce hint reliance, which measures how strongly correct hinted trajectories depend on the hint. We derive a transferability result showing that lower hint reliance implies stronger transfer from hinted success to no-hint success, and we use this result to define a transfer-weighted reward for training the hinter. Therefore, HiLL favors hints that not only recover informative GRPO groups, but also produce signals that are more likely to improve the original no-hint policy. Experiments across multiple benchmarks show that HiLL consistently outperforms GRPO and prior hint-based baselines, demonstrating the value of adaptive and transfer-aware hint learning for RL. The code is available at https://github.com/Andree-9/HiLL.

ru.title: Адаптивные подсказки для улучшения сигналов обучения в обучении с подкреплением
ru.desc: В статье предлагается фреймворк HiLL для обучения с подкреплением, который решает проблему коллапса преимущества в групповой относительной оптимизации политики. Система совместно обучает политику генерации подсказок и политику рассуждения, где подсказки генерируются адаптивно на основе ошибок текущей модели. Авторы вводят метрику надежности подсказок, которая показывает, насколько успешные траектории зависят от подсказки, и доказывают, что низкая надежность улучшает трансфер знаний на задачи без подсказок. Эксперименты на нескольких бенчмарках демонстрируют, что адаптивное и ориентированное на трансфер обучение подсказкам существенно превосходит базовые методы оптимизации политики.

en.title: Adaptive Hints for Enhanced Learning in Reinforcement Learning
en.desc: HiLL is a novel reinforcement learning framework that generates adaptive hints based on the errors made by a reasoner, enhancing the learning process. It addresses the problem of advantage collapse in Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO), where uniform rewards lead to no learning signals. By training a hint generator alongside the reasoner, HiLL ensures that hints are tailored to the specific challenges faced by the reasoner, improving the overall learning outcome. The framework also introduces the concept of hint reliance, which helps in measuring the effectiveness of hints in transferring learning to scenarios without hints, ultimately leading to better performance in reinforcement learning tasks.

zh.title: 自适应提示学习，提升强化学习效果
zh.desc: HiLL是一种强化学习框架，能够根据推理器的错误自适应地生成提示，以改善学习信号和在群体相对策略优化中的转移性能。传统的群体相对策略优化（GRPO）在面对相同奖励时容易导致优势崩溃，进而无法提供有效的学习信号。HiLL通过在线生成提示，帮助推理器在面对困难问题时产生多样化的结果，从而恢复非零更新。实验结果表明，HiLL在多个基准测试中优于GRPO和其他基于提示的方法，展示了自适应和转移感知的提示学习在强化学习中的重要性。

papers.title: FP4 Explore, BF16 Train: Diffusion Reinforcement Learning via Efficient Rollout Scaling
papers.abstract: Abstract A novel two-stage reinforcement learning framework called Sol-RL integrates FP4 quantization with diffusion model alignment to accelerate training while maintaining high-fidelity performance.  AI-generated summary Reinforcement-Learning-based post-training has recently emerged as a promising paradigm for aligning text-to-image diffusion models with human preferences. In recent studies, increasing the rollout group size yields pronounced performance improvements, indicating substantial room for further alignment gains. However, scaling rollouts on large-scale foundational diffusion models (e.g., FLUX.1-12B) imposes a heavy computational burden. To alleviate this bottleneck, we explore the integration of FP4 quantization into Diffusion RL rollouts. Yet, we identify that naive quantized pipelines inherently introduce risks of performance degradation. To overcome this dilemma between efficiency and training integrity, we propose Sol-RL (Speed-of-light RL), a novel FP4-empowered Two-stage Reinforcement Learning framework. First, we utilize high-throughput NVFP4 rollouts to generate a massive candidate pool and extract a highly contrastive subset. Second, we regenerate these selected samples in BF16 precision and optimize the policy exclusively on them. By decoupling candidate exploration from policy optimization, Sol-RL integrates the algorithmic mechanisms of rollout scaling with the system-level throughput gains of NVFP4. This synergistic algorithm-hardware design effectively accelerates the rollout phase while reserving high-fidelity samples for optimization. We empirically demonstrate that our framework maintains the training integrity of BF16 precision pipeline while fully exploiting the throughput gains enabled by FP4 arithmetic. Extensive experiments across SANA, FLUX.1, and SD3.5-L substantiate that our approach delivers superior alignment performance across multiple metrics while accelerating training convergence by up to 4.64times, unlocking the power of massive rollout scaling at a fraction of the cost.

ru.title: Квантизация для ускорения обучения диффузионных моделей с подкреплением
ru.desc: В статье представлена Sol-RL — двухэтапная система обучения с подкреплением для выравнивания диффузионных моделей генерации изображений. На первом этапе используется квантизация FP4 для быстрого создания большого пула кандидатов, из которого извлекаются наиболее информативные примеры. На втором этапе эти отобранные примеры регенерируются в высокой точности BF16 и используются для оптимизации политики модели. Такой двухуровневый подход позволяет ускорить обучение в 4.64 раза, сохраняя качество выравнивания с человеческими предпочтениями.

en.title: Accelerating Reinforcement Learning with Sol-RL: Fast and Accurate!
en.desc: The paper presents Sol-RL, a two-stage reinforcement learning framework that combines FP4 quantization with diffusion model alignment to enhance training efficiency without sacrificing performance. It addresses the challenge of scaling rollout sizes in large diffusion models, which can be computationally expensive. By separating the candidate generation and policy optimization processes, Sol-RL leverages high-throughput NVFP4 rollouts to create a diverse candidate pool and then refines a selected subset in BF16 precision. The results show that this approach significantly improves alignment performance and accelerates training convergence, achieving up to 4.64 times faster training while maintaining high-quality outputs.

zh.title: 高效训练与高保真并存的Sol-RL框架
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新颖的两阶段强化学习框架，称为Sol-RL，结合了FP4量化和扩散模型对齐，以加速训练并保持高保真性能。通过后训练的强化学习方法，能够更好地将文本到图像的扩散模型与人类偏好对齐。我们发现，增加回放组的大小可以显著提高性能，但在大规模基础扩散模型上进行扩展会带来巨大的计算负担。Sol-RL通过将候选样本的探索与策略优化解耦，利用高通量的NVFP4回放生成大量候选池，从而有效加速回放阶段，同时保留高保真样本进行优化。

papers.title: The Depth Ceiling: On the Limits of Large Language Models in Discovering Latent Planning
papers.abstract: Abstract Research reveals that large language models can perform latent reasoning with varying depths, but there's a gap between discovering and executing multi-step planning strategies, suggesting limitations for chain-of-thought monitoring.  AI-generated summary The viability of chain-of-thought (CoT) monitoring hinges on models being unable to reason effectively in their latent representations. Yet little is known about the limits of such latent reasoning in LLMs. We test these limits by studying whether models can discover multi-step planning strategies without supervision on intermediate steps and execute them latently, within a single forward pass. Using graph path-finding tasks that precisely control the number of required latent planning steps, we uncover a striking limitation unresolved by massive scaling: tiny transformers trained from scratch discover strategies requiring up to three latent steps, fine-tuned GPT-4o and Qwen3-32B reach five, and GPT-5.4 attains seven under few-shot prompting. Although the maximum latent planning depth models can learn during training is five, the discovered strategy generalizes up to eight latent steps at test-time. This reveals a dissociation between the ability to discover a latent strategy under final-answer supervision alone and the ability to execute it once discovered. If similar limits hold more broadly, strategies requiring multiple coordinated latent planning steps may need to be explicitly taught or externalized, lending credence to CoT monitoring.

ru.title: Скрытое мышление моделей имеет пределы: от открытия стратегии к её исполнению
ru.desc: В исследовании показано, что большие языковые модели способны выполнять скрытое рассуждение различной глубины, но существует разрыв между обнаружением и исполнением многошаговых стратегий планирования. Авторы тестируют пределы скрытого рассуждения на задачах поиска пути в графе, которые точно контролируют количество необходимых шагов планирования. Они обнаруживают фундаментальное ограничение: маленькие трансформеры обучаются открывать стратегии до трёх шагов, GPT-4o и Qwen3-32B достигают пяти шагов, а GPT-5.4 при few-shot промптинге достигает семи. Исследование выявляет диссоциацию между способностью модели обнаружить скрытую стратегию и способностью её исполнить, что говорит в пользу явного обучения многошаговому планированию.

en.title: Unlocking the Limits of Latent Reasoning in Language Models
en.desc: This paper investigates the reasoning capabilities of large language models (LLMs) in executing multi-step planning strategies. It highlights a gap in the models' ability to discover and implement these strategies without supervision on intermediate steps. The research shows that while models can learn to plan up to five latent steps, they can generalize to eight steps during testing. This suggests that for complex reasoning tasks, explicit teaching or external guidance may be necessary to enhance chain-of-thought monitoring.

zh.title: 揭示潜在推理的局限性
zh.desc: 这篇论文研究了大型语言模型在潜在推理方面的能力，发现它们在多步骤规划策略的发现与执行之间存在差距。研究表明，模型在没有监督的情况下，能够发现最多三到七个潜在步骤的策略，但在执行时却受到限制。尽管模型在训练中能够学习到最多五个潜在步骤的规划深度，但在测试时发现的策略可以推广到八个步骤。这表明，仅依靠最终答案的监督，模型在发现潜在策略和执行策略之间存在脱节。

papers.title: VenusBench-Mobile: A Challenging and User-Centric Benchmark for Mobile GUI Agents with Capability Diagnostics
papers.abstract: Abstract VenusBench-Mobile presents a comprehensive evaluation framework for mobile GUI agents that reveals significant performance gaps compared to existing benchmarks, emphasizing the need for more robust real-world deployment capabilities.  AI-generated summary Existing online benchmarks for mobile GUI agents remain largely app-centric and task-homogeneous, failing to reflect the diversity and instability of real-world mobile usage. To this end, we introduce VenusBench-Mobile, a challenging online benchmark for evaluating general-purpose mobile GUI agents under realistic, user-centric conditions. VenusBench-Mobile builds two core evaluation pillars: defining what to evaluate via user-intent-driven task design that reflects real mobile usage, and how to evaluate through a capability-oriented annotation scheme for fine-grained agent behavior analysis. Extensive evaluation of state-of-the-art mobile GUI agents reveals large performance gaps relative to prior benchmarks, indicating that VenusBench-Mobile poses substantially more challenging and realistic tasks and that current agents remain far from reliable real-world deployment. Diagnostic analysis further shows that failures are dominated by deficiencies in perception and memory, which are largely obscured by coarse-grained evaluations. Moreover, even the strongest agents exhibit near-zero success under environment variations, highlighting their brittleness in realistic settings. Based on these insights, we believe VenusBench-Mobile provides an important stepping stone toward robust real-world deployment of mobile GUI agents. Code and data are available at https://github.com/inclusionAI/UI-Venus/tree/VenusBench-Mobile.

ru.title: Путь к надежным мобильным агентам через реалистичную оценку
ru.desc: VenusBench-Mobile представляет комплексную систему оценки для агентов, управляющих мобильным интерфейсом, которая выявляет значительные пробелы в производительности по сравнению с существующими бенчмарками. Авторы создали требовательный онлайн-бенчмарк, основанный на реальных сценариях использования мобильных приложений, с ориентацией на намерения пользователей вместо узкоспециализированных задач. Оценка современных моделей выявила критические недостатки в восприятии и памяти агентов, которые были замаскированы грубыми метриками оценки. Результаты подчеркивают хрупкость текущих решений в реальных условиях и указывают на необходимость разработки более устойчивых систем для практического применения.

en.title: Bridging the Gap: Real-World Evaluation for Mobile GUI Agents
en.desc: VenusBench-Mobile is a new evaluation framework designed to assess mobile GUI agents in real-world scenarios. It highlights the shortcomings of existing benchmarks that focus too much on specific apps and tasks, which do not represent the varied and unpredictable nature of actual mobile usage. The framework introduces user-intent-driven task design and a detailed annotation scheme to better analyze agent performance. Findings show that current mobile GUI agents struggle significantly in realistic conditions, particularly due to issues with perception and memory, indicating a need for improvement before they can be reliably deployed in everyday environments.

zh.title: 提升移动GUI代理的真实世界能力
zh.desc: VenusBench-Mobile是一个全面的评估框架，专门用于移动图形用户界面（GUI）代理的性能测试。该框架揭示了现有基准测试与真实世界应用之间的显著性能差距，强调了需要更强大的实际部署能力。VenusBench-Mobile通过用户意图驱动的任务设计和能力导向的注释方案，定义了评估的内容和方式，反映了真实的移动使用情况。研究表明，当前的移动GUI代理在面对真实环境变化时表现脆弱，远未达到可靠的实际应用标准。

papers.title: Q-Zoom: Query-Aware Adaptive Perception for Efficient Multimodal Large Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Q-Zoom enhances MLLM performance by adaptively focusing computational resources on relevant visual regions through dynamic gating and self-distilled region proposal networks, achieving faster inference without sacrificing accuracy.  AI-generated summary MLLMs require high-resolution visual inputs for fine-grained tasks like document understanding and dense scene perception. However, current global resolution scaling paradigms indiscriminately flood the quadratic self-attention mechanism with visually redundant tokens, severely bottlenecking inference throughput while ignoring spatial sparsity and query intent. To overcome this, we propose Q-Zoom, a query-aware adaptive high-resolution perception framework that operates in an efficient coarse-to-fine manner. First, a lightweight Dynamic Gating Network safely bypasses high-resolution processing when coarse global features suffice. Second, for queries demanding fine-grained perception, a Self-Distilled Region Proposal Network (SD-RPN) precisely localizes the task-relevant Region-of-Interest (RoI) directly from intermediate feature spaces. To optimize these modules efficiently, the gating network uses a consistency-aware generation strategy to derive deterministic routing labels, while the SD-RPN employs a fully self-supervised distillation paradigm. A continuous spatio-temporal alignment scheme and targeted fine-tuning then seamlessly fuse the dense local RoI with the coarse global layout. Extensive experiments demonstrate that Q-Zoom establishes a dominant Pareto frontier. Using Qwen2.5-VL-7B as a primary testbed, Q-Zoom accelerates inference by 2.52 times on Document & OCR benchmarks and 4.39 times in High-Resolution scenarios while matching the baseline's peak accuracy. Furthermore, when configured for maximum perceptual fidelity, Q-Zoom surpasses the baseline's peak performance by 1.1% and 8.1% on these respective benchmarks. These robust improvements transfer seamlessly to Qwen3-VL, LLaVA, and emerging RL-based thinking-with-image models. Project page is available at https://yuhengsss.github.io/Q-Zoom/.
affiliations: City University of Hong Kong, China

ru.title: Адаптивное сжатие вычислений: обработка только нужных частей изображения
ru.desc: Q-Zoom — это фреймворк для оптимизации мультимодальных больших языковых моделей (MLLM), который адаптивно распределяет вычислительные ресурсы на релевантные области изображения. Система использует динамическую сеть гейтинга для определения необходимости обработки высокого разрешения и самодистиллированную сеть предложения регионов для точной локализации интересующих областей. Подход реализует эффективную двухэтапную стратегию: сначала извлекаются грубые глобальные признаки, затем для сложных задач выделяются и обрабатываются детальные локальные регионы. Экспериментальные результаты показывают ускорение вывода в 2.5-4.4 раза без потери точности, с потенциалом улучшения качества на некоторых бенчмарках.

en.title: Q-Zoom: Smart Resource Allocation for Faster MLLM Inference
en.desc: Q-Zoom is a novel framework designed to enhance the performance of Multi-Modal Large Language Models (MLLMs) by intelligently directing computational resources to the most relevant visual areas. It employs a Dynamic Gating Network to efficiently manage high-resolution processing, allowing for faster inference without compromising accuracy. Additionally, the Self-Distilled Region Proposal Network (SD-RPN) focuses on accurately identifying important regions of interest for tasks requiring detailed visual understanding. Through extensive testing, Q-Zoom has demonstrated significant improvements in inference speed and accuracy across various benchmarks, making it a powerful tool for high-resolution visual tasks.

zh.title: Q-Zoom：高效的视觉区域聚焦框架
zh.desc: Q-Zoom 是一种增强 MLLM 性能的框架，通过动态门控和自蒸馏区域提议网络，智能地将计算资源集中在相关的视觉区域，从而实现更快的推理速度而不牺牲准确性。该方法解决了当前全局分辨率缩放策略带来的冗余视觉信息问题，避免了自注意力机制的瓶颈。Q-Zoom 采用查询感知的自适应高分辨率感知框架，首先通过轻量级动态门控网络在全局特征足够时跳过高分辨率处理。对于需要细粒度感知的查询，自蒸馏区域提议网络能够从中间特征空间精确定位任务相关的兴趣区域，优化了推理效率。

papers.title: MoRight: Motion Control Done Right
papers.abstract: Abstract MoRight is a unified framework that enables disentangled motion control and causal relationship modeling in video generation, allowing separate manipulation of object motion and camera viewpoint while ensuring physically plausible interactions.  AI-generated summary Generating motion-controlled videos--where user-specified actions drive physically plausible scene dynamics under freely chosen viewpoints--demands two capabilities: (1) disentangled motion control, allowing users to separately control the object motion and adjust camera viewpoint; and (2) motion causality, ensuring that user-driven actions trigger coherent reactions from other objects rather than merely displacing pixels. Existing methods fall short on both fronts: they entangle camera and object motion into a single tracking signal and treat motion as kinematic displacement without modeling causal relationships between object motion. We introduce MoRight, a unified framework that addresses both limitations through disentangled motion modeling. Object motion is specified in a canonical static-view and transferred to an arbitrary target camera viewpoint via temporal cross-view attention, enabling disentangled camera and object control. We further decompose motion into active (user-driven) and passive (consequence) components, training the model to learn motion causality from data. At inference, users can either supply active motion and MoRight predicts consequences (forward reasoning), or specify desired passive outcomes and MoRight recovers plausible driving actions (inverse reasoning), all while freely adjusting the camera viewpoint. Experiments on three benchmarks demonstrate state-of-the-art performance in generation quality, motion controllability, and interaction awareness.

ru.title: Разделённое управление движением и причинностью в видеогенерации
ru.desc: MoRight — это унифицированная система для генерации видео с контролем движения, которая разделяет управление движением объектов и ракурсом камеры. Фреймворк моделирует причинно-следственные связи между объектами, разложив движение на активные (управляемые пользователем) и пассивные (следствия) компоненты. Система использует кросс-видовое внимание для переноса движения из статического вида в произвольный ракурс камеры. Модель поддерживает как прямое рассуждение (предсказание последствий), так и обратное (восстановление действий для желаемого результата).

en.title: Disentangled Motion Control for Realistic Video Generation
en.desc: MoRight is a novel framework designed for generating videos where users can control object motion and camera angles independently. It achieves this by disentangling motion control, allowing for separate manipulation of how objects move and how the camera views them. The framework also incorporates motion causality, ensuring that actions taken by users lead to realistic reactions from other objects in the scene. Through advanced techniques like temporal cross-view attention, MoRight enhances the quality of generated videos while maintaining coherent interactions among elements in the scene.

zh.title: 解耦运动控制与因果建模的统一框架
zh.desc: MoRight是一个统一框架，旨在视频生成中实现解耦的运动控制和因果关系建模。它允许用户独立操控物体运动和相机视角，同时确保物理上合理的交互。该框架通过时间跨视图注意力将物体运动从静态视图转移到任意目标相机视角，解决了现有方法在运动控制和因果关系建模上的不足。实验结果表明，MoRight在生成质量、运动可控性和交互意识方面达到了最先进的性能。

papers.title: Tunable Soft Equivariance with Guarantees
papers.abstract: Abstract A general framework for constructing soft equivariant models through weight projection into designed subspaces is proposed, demonstrating improved performance and reduced equivariance error across multiple computer vision tasks.  AI-generated summary Equivariance is a fundamental property in computer vision models, yet strict equivariance is rarely satisfied in real-world data, which can limit a model's performance. Controlling the degree of equivariance is therefore desirable. We propose a general framework for constructing soft equivariant models by projecting the model weights into a designed subspace. The method applies to any pre-trained architecture and provides theoretical bounds on the induced equivariance error. Empirically, we demonstrate the effectiveness of our method on multiple pre-trained backbones, including ViT and ResNet, across image classification, semantic segmentation, and human-trajectory prediction tasks. Notably, our approach improves the performance while simultaneously reducing equivariance error on the competitive ImageNet benchmark.

ru.title: Мягкая эквивариантность: контролируемое управление инвариантностью моделей
ru.desc: В статье предлагается общий метод создания моделей с контролируемой эквивариантностью путём проектирования весов нейросети в специально разработанные подпространства. Авторы показывают, что строгая эквивариантность редко достигается в реальных данных и может ограничивать производительность моделей. Метод применим к любой предварительно обученной архитектуре и включает теоретические оценки ошибки эквивариантности. Экспериментальная валидация на ImageNet и других задачах компьютерного зрения подтверждает, что подход одновременно улучшает производительность и снижает ошибку эквивариантности.

en.title: Enhancing Performance with Soft Equivariance in Vision Models
en.desc: This paper introduces a new way to create soft equivariant models in computer vision by projecting model weights into specific subspaces. Equivariance is important for models to perform well, but real-world data often doesn't meet strict equivariance requirements. The proposed framework allows for controlling how much equivariance is present, which can enhance model performance. The authors show that their method works well with various pre-trained models like ViT and ResNet, improving results in tasks such as image classification and semantic segmentation while reducing equivariance errors.

zh.title: 构建软等变模型的新框架
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种通过将权重投影到设计的子空间中构建软等变模型的通用框架。这种方法可以应用于任何预训练的架构，并提供了理论上的等变误差界限。通过在多个计算机视觉任务上进行实证验证，我们展示了该方法在图像分类、语义分割和人类轨迹预测等任务中的有效性。特别是在竞争激烈的ImageNet基准测试中，我们的方法不仅提高了性能，同时减少了等变误差。
